DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. ROBERT S. JARET, Esq. of Jaret & Jaret (Tel: 415-455-1010) and ARTHUR R. SIEGEL, Esq. of Law Offices of Arthur R. Siegel (Tel: 415-395-9335) for plaintiff Eugene Chelberg;
Atty. ANGELA S. CHUANG, Esq. of Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton LLP (Tel: 213-620-1780) for defendant the Board of Trustees of the California State University.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's demurrer to the second amended complaint (SAC) is overruled. The demurrer attacks only one of the SAC's seven counts, the second. Defendant says plaintiff's "harassment claim fails" because its allegations "arise solely from personnel management actions or innocuous conduct, which is neither severe, nor pervasive." (Memo. 5:6-9.) The court disagrees. Taken as true (as required on demurrer), the pled allegations suffice for a harassment claim - the trier of fact could find them severe, pervasive and not innocuous. (See SAC 4:25-8:15, 11:1-13:20.) Moreover, affixing a "personnel management action" label to misconduct does not immunize it from a harassment claim. (Roby v. McKesson Corp. (2009) 47 Cal.4th 686, 707-08.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
